name: codenames-parser
documentation: # Codenames Parser [![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/codenames-parser.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/codenames-parser) [![Pipeline](https://github.com/asaf-kali/codenames-parser/actions/workflows/pipeline.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/asaf-kali/codenames-parser/actions/workflows/pipeline.yml) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/github/asaf-kali/codenames-parser/graph/badge.svg?token=HET5E8P1UK)](https://codecov.io/github/asaf-kali/codenames-parser) [![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) [![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-111111.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black) [![Imports: isort](https://img.shields.io/badge/imports-isort-%231674b1)](https://pycqa.github.io/isort/) [![Type checked: mypy](https://img.shields.io/badge/type%20check-mypy-22aa11)](http://mypy-lang.org/) [![Linting: pylint](https://img.shields.io/badge/linting-pylint-22aa11)](https://github.com/pylint-dev/pylint) A Python package to parse Codenames game boards from images.\ Before we dive in, here are some examples: ### Color map extraction Given `color_map.png`: \ \ Running: ``` python -m codenames_parser/color_map/entrypoint.py color_map.png classic ``` Outputs: ``` Some parsing logs... # As emoji table ⬜ 🟥 🟦 🟦 🟥 ⬜ 🟦 🟥 ⬜ 🟦 🟦 🟥 🟥 🟦 ⬜ 🟦 🟥 🟥 ⬜ ⬜ ⬜ 🟦 💀 🟥 🟦 # As list ['NEUTRAL', 'RED', 'BLUE', 'BLUE', 'RED', 'NEUTRAL', 'BLUE', 'RED', 'NEUTRAL', 'BLUE', 'BLUE', 'RED', 'RED', 'BLUE', 'NEUTRAL', 'BLUE', 'RED', 'RED', 'NEUTRAL', 'NEUTRAL', 'NEUTRAL', 'BLUE', 'ASSASSIN', 'RED', 'BLUE'] ``` ### Board extraction "Life is not perfect, neither is OCR" (credit: Github Copilot) Given `board.png`: \ \ Running: ``` python -m codenames_parser/board/entrypoint.py board.png heb ``` Outputs: ``` Some parsing logs... # As table +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ | ציבור | אוטובוס | ישראל | מתח | גס | +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ | ברית | גוש | איום | מורח | קנה | +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ | לידה | מבחן | אודם | שוקו | חטיף | +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ | חוק | רץ | חצות | רדיו | כתם | +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ | גרם | כהן | רושם | אלמוג | | +-------+---------+-------+-------+------+ # As list [ "ציבור", "אוטובוס", "ישראל", "מתח", "גס", "ברית", "גוש", "איום", "מורח", "קנה", "לידה", "מבחן", "אודם", "שוקו", "חטיף", "חוק", "רץ", "חצות", "רדיו", "כתם", "גרם", "כהן", "רושם", "אלמוג", "", ] ``` \* It looks as if the direction of the board is flipped. This is a bug due to Hebrew being written from right to left, the list order is correct. ## Installation `pip install codenames-parser` ### OCR 1. Uses `pytesseract` to extract text from images. 2. Requires `tesseract` to be installed on the system ( see [installing-tesseract](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#installing-tesseract)). 3. Download more languages from: https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdata

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.mask.color_distance_negative
documentation:
Calculates the negative of the color distance.
i.e., the closer the pixel is to the color, the higher the value.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.mask.apply_mask
documentation: Applies a mask to an image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.color_detection.classify_cell_colors
documentation: Classifies the color of each cell by clustering their average colors.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.color_detection._get_optimal_k
documentation: Returns the optimal number of clusters for the given color type.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.color_detection.assign_colors_to_clusters
documentation: Assigns CardColor to each cluster based on the closest CODENAMES color.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.grid_detection.extract_cells
documentation: pylint: disable=R0801

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.grid_detection._detect_all_boxes
documentation:
Given deduplicated boxes and their distances from the average center, in iteration:
    1. all_boxes = Complete missing boxes
        1.a If there are intersections between boxes in all_boxes, this is not a valid grid, break the loop.
    2. Calculate area fit (the intersection between boxes and all_boxes)
        2.a If the area fit is too low, remove the box with the highest distance and go back to step 1.
        2.b If the area fit is good, return.

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.grid_detection._calculate_area_fit
documentation: the total intersection between boxes and all_boxes

functions.name: codenames_parser.color_map.grid_detection._is_legal_grid
documentation:
Check if the grid is legal by checking if there are intersections between boxes.
Assume boxes are exactly the same size, and in constant distances.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.scale.scale_down_image
documentation: Scale down the image to a maximum dimension of max_dimension.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.scale.downsample_image
documentation:
Downsample the image by the given factor.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): Input image.
    factor (float): Downsampling factor.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Downsampled image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.padding.value_pad
documentation:
Pad the image with a constant value on all sides.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): Input image.
    padding (int): Padding size.
    value (int): Padding value.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Padded image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.padding.border_pad
documentation:
Pad the image with the value of the closest border pixel.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): Input image.
    padding (int): Padding size.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Padded image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.reader.read_image
documentation:
Reads an image from a file.

Args:
    image_path (str): Path to the image file.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The image in BGR format.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.crop.crop_image
documentation:
Crop the input image according to the main Hough lines.

Args:
    image: The input image.
    min_crop_ratio: The minimum ratio of the cropped image to the original image.
    Meaning, if the cropped image is smaller than the original image by this ratio, the cropping is skipped.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.crop.crop_by_bounds
documentation: Crop the input image according to the given bounds.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.crop.crop_by_box
documentation: Crop the input image according to the given box.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.crop.find_crop_bounds
documentation: Find the crop bounds for the given axis.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.crop.rotated_crop
documentation:
Crop a region from the image, taking rotation into account.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): Original source image.
    angle (float): Rotation angle of the region.
    top_left (Point): Top-left corner location of the region.
    size (tuple[int, int]): Size of the region (height, width).

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Cropped and straightened matched region from the source image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.perspective_correction.correct_perspective
documentation:
Corrects the perspective of the image to get a top-down view of the board.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): The input image.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The perspective-corrected image.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.perspective_correction.order_points
documentation:
Orders points in the order: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left.

Args:
    pts (np.ndarray): An array of four points.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The ordered points.

functions.name: codenames_parser.common.impr.perspective_correction.four_point_transform
documentation:
Performs a perspective transform to obtain a top-down view.

Args:
    image (np.ndarray): The input image.
    pts (np.ndarray): The corner points of the board.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The warped image.

class_variables.name: codenames_parser.common.utils.models.Line.rho
documentation: distance from the origin

class_variables.name: codenames_parser.common.utils.models.Line.theta
documentation: angle in radians

modules.name: codenames_parser.board.board_parser
documentation: pylint: disable=R0801

functions.name: codenames_parser.board.grid_detection._complete_missing_boxes
documentation:
Complete missing boxes in the list to reach the expected GRID_SIZE.
Boxes might not be exactly aligned in a grid, so we can't assume constant row and column sizes.
We need to understand which boxes are missing, and then try to assume their positions,
based on the found boxes in their row and column.

functions.name: codenames_parser.board.grid_detection._predict_missing_boxes_centers
documentation: Predict the centers of missing boxes by looking at the average centers of its row and column.

functions.name: codenames_parser.board.grid_detection._get_row_col_indices
documentation: Get the indices of all boxes in the same row and column as the given box index.

functions.name: codenames_parser.board.template_search.search_template
documentation:
Search for the template location in the source image using pyramid search.

Args:
    source (np.ndarray): Source image.
    template (np.ndarray): Template image.
    num_iterations (int, optional): Number of iterations.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Matched region from the source image.

modules.name: codenames_parser.board.entrypoint
documentation: pylint: disable=R0801
